title: Stenostomum aromaticum
taxonomy: Stenostomum aromaticum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a part of the tribe Spermacoceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "beardtongue" or "spermacoce" group. The tribe Spermacoceae is further classified under the subfamily Rubioideae, which is a subfamily of Rubiaceae. The classification of Stenostomum aromaticum is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The classification is subject to change as new research and findings become available.

side_effects: Stenostomum aromaticum, also known as the "wild rosemary," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. As with many plants, Stenostomum aromaticum may cause allergic reactions or skin irritation in some individuals, especially when handling its leaves or essential oil extracts.
